frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: Yaar, I really feel women generalize too much sometimes. Like, not all men are bad, right? It's unfair to expect us 'good guys' to constantly be on edge or prove ourselves because of a few idiots.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment dismisses women's generalized concerns about male behavior by centering the speaker's feeling of being unfairly blamed as a 'good guy.' It directly uses the 'not all men' argument and frames women's wariness as an irrational overreaction ('generalize too much').
original_frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 38. Married. A man in Mumbai. You are on a WhatsApp call with your younger brother who lives in Pune. Your brother just told you that his girlfriend wants to wear a western dress to their family puja next month instead of a saree or salwar. Your brother is unsure how to handle it. You are giving him your advice on what to tell her and why it matters what she wears to a family religious function and what the family will think.
